Chemotherapy regimens containing bleomycin are contraindicated because of unacceptable pulmonary toxicity [6].

For metastatic disease, first-line options include TIP (preferred), cisplatin/fluorouracil, or platinum/fluorouracil + pembrolizumab followed by maintenance pembrolizumab. Subsequent-line: clinical trial preferred, or pembrolizumab if MSI-H/dMMR/TMB-H, or paclitaxel/cetuximab [PN-D].

The HERCULES (LACOG 0218) trial demonstrated an ORR of 39.4% with pembrolizumab + platinum/fluorouracil as first-line therapy in advanced penile cancer, with median PFS 5.4 months and OS 9.6 months [5], [173].
Bilateral PLND is indicated when ≥4 positive inguinal nodes are present (total both sides) [141].
Surveillance imaging intensity depends on nodal status: for pN2/N3, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis every 3–6 months for first 2–4 years; for pN0/N1, less frequent [PN-8].

key_trials.full_name: Neoadjuvant paclitaxel, ifosfamide, and cisplatin chemotherapy for metastatic penile cancer: a phase II study
key_trials.population: Patients with stage N2 or N3 (stage III or IV) penile cancer without distant metastases
key_trials.intervention: Neoadjuvant TIP (paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 IV day 1, ifosfamide 1200 mg/m2 IV days 1-3, cisplatin 25 mg/m2 IV days 1-3) every 3-4 weeks for 4 courses, followed by consolidative surgery
key_trials.key_result: ORR 50%; 22 patients (73.3%) underwent surgery. Estimated long-term progression-free survival for intent-to-treat was 36.7%. Improved PFS and OS associated with objective response to chemotherapy (P < .001 and P = .001).
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Absence of bilateral residual tumor, extranodal extension, and skin involvement were associated with better outcomes.
key_trials.practice_change: Established TIP as preferred neoadjuvant regimen for bulky inguinal or unresectable penile cancer. Still standard of care.

key_trials.full_name: Pembrolizumab Plus Platinum-Based Chemotherapy for Patients With Advanced Penile Cancer: The Nonrandomized HERCULES (LACOG 0218) Clinical Trial
key_trials.population: Patients with locally advanced or metastatic penile SCC, first-line systemic therapy
key_trials.intervention: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 IV day 1 (or carboplatin AUC 5 IV day 1) + continuous infusion fluorouracil 1000 mg/m2/day days 1-4 + pembrolizumab 200 mg IV day 1 every 3 weeks for 6 cycles, followed by pembrolizumab maintenance 200 mg IV every 3 weeks up to 34 cycles
key_trials.key_result: ORR 39.4% (1 complete response, 12 partial responses). Median PFS 5.4 months, median OS 9.6 months at median follow-up 24 months. Manageable safety profile.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Safety, duration of response, overall survival.
key_trials.practice_change: Introduced pembrolizumab + platinum/fluorouracil as a first-line treatment option for advanced penile cancer (category 2A).

key_trials.full_name: Efficacy of Pembrolizumab in Patients With Noncolorectal High Microsatellite Instability/Mismatch Repair-Deficient Cancer (cohort) and Association of Tumour Mutational Burden with Outcomes
key_trials.journal: Lancet Oncology (TMB analysis) and Journal of Clinical Oncology (MSI-H/dMMR analysis)
key_trials.n: 233 (MSI-H/dMMR cohort for efficacy) and 790 evaluable for TMB (TMB-H n=102)"
key_trials.population: Patients with previously treated advanced noncolorectal MSI-H/dMMR solid tumors (for the MSI-H analysis) and patients with advanced solid tumors for TMB analysis
key_trials.intervention: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV every 3 weeks for up to 2 years
key_trials.primary_endpoint: ORR (MSI-H/dMMR cohort); ORR by TMB status (exploratory)
key_trials.key_result: MSI-H/dMMR: ORR 34.3%, median PFS 4.1 months, median OS 23.5 months; grade ≥3 AEs 14.6%, one treatment-related death (pneumonia). TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb): ORR 29% vs 6% for non-TMB-H.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Disease control rate, duration of response, safety.
key_trials.practice_change: Supported tumor-agnostic FDA approvals of pembrolizumab for MSI-H/dMMR and TMB-H solid tumors. Used in penile cancer for subsequent-line therapy.

key_trials.population: Patients with locally advanced penile cancer (stage III/IV) with inguinal node metastases
key_trials.intervention: Two sequential randomizations: 1) Neoadjuvant randomization: ILND alone vs neoadjuvant chemotherapy vs neoadjuvant chemoradiotherapy; 2) Pelvic randomization: prophylactic PLND vs surveillance with adjuvant chemoradiotherapy for high-risk features
key_trials.key_result: Trial not yet reported; accrual ongoing.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Progression-free survival, quality of life, toxicity.
key_trials.practice_change: Will define role of neoadjuvant therapy and prophylactic PLND in node-positive penile cancer.
